claim_tested: A committed Understand-Anything graph contains enough structured evidence to create one coherent narrated, subtitled, deterministic proof video.
limitations: Internal technical experiment, not external customer or learner evidence
The source graph is stale relative to the selected upstream implementation commit
The 56.592-second proof uses 1280x720 rather than the target 1920x1080 output
The proof subtitle cue can render beyond the production two-line limit
The proof does not exercise GPT-5.6 runtime planning or an external production speech provider

measurements.metric: Grounded proof videos produced from a validated graph

provenance.collection_method: System.Speech synthesis, deterministic FFmpeg render, ffprobe stream inspection, and SHA-256 hashing

sample_context.description: One bounded local graph-to-video feasibility spike using the upstream dashboard sample graph and offline English system speech
sample_context.population: One stale sample graph; no learner population
